REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ANDREW IWACH NOTICE OF MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS TO PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE SECOND AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. MOTION GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION (TO ALLEGE STANDING) AND AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION (TO RE-ALLEGE AS COGNIZABLE CAUSE OF ACTION). THE QUESTION OF NUISANCE IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. DEFENDANT TO SUBMITT A PROPOSED ORDER CONFORMING WITH THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH LANGUAGE REGARDING THE QUESTION OF NUISANCE; OVERRULED OR SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BARBARA OLDER, DYER STICKNEY, LYLE OLDER's Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint is GRANTED with leave to amend to allege specific facts sufficient to state a claim for punitive damages and attorney fees.. Motion is GRANTED As follows: (1) as to page 15, line 6 "malicious", page 18, lines 1-2 "for punitive damages pursuant to California Civil Code, Section 3294", and (2) as to page 18, lines 5-6: "For attorney fees and litigations costs pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1021.5." Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Newton J. Lam, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Brett Gibbs, (415)296-7070 for Defendant/Moving party Andrew Iwach.
Ashley Chinen, (415)926-7628 for Plaintiff/Opposing party Porcia's LLC. (via courtcall)

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ANDREW IWACH Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings To Plaintiffs Complaint is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to the second and fourth causes of action. Motion GRANTED without leave to amend as to the first and third causes of action. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action (to allege standing) and as to the sixth cause of action (to re-allege as cognizable cause of action). The question of nuisance is Taken Under Submission. Defendant to submitt a proposed order conforming with the tentative ruling with language regarding the question of nuisance; overruled or sustained with leave to amend. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
